channel.title: La Cyber Sphère — Bulletin de veille cyber
channel.description: Alertes critiques et élevées, résumées et sourcées.

item.title: [Faible] Windows KB5121767 OOB update fixes shutdowns on some Dell PCs
item.description: Microsoft has released emergency updates to fix a known issue causing some Dell PCs to shut down after installing the July 2026 Windows 11 security updates. [...]

item.title: [Critique] Critical ServiceNow code execution flaw now exploited in attacks
item.description: Attackers have begun exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-6875) in the ServiceNow AI Platform, according to threat intelligence company Defused. [...]

item.title: [Moyen] New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
item.description: Opening a crafted XZ archive in 7-Zip could let an attacker run code on the machine. The flaw, CVE-2026-14266, is a heap-based buffer overflow in how the archiver processes XZ chunked data, and Trend Micro's Zero Day…

item.title: [Critique] Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
item.description:
A solo Russian-speaking threat actor known as "bandcampro" outsourced a chunk of their operations to Google's open-source Gemini CLI artificial intelligence (AI) and commandeered a live botnet.
The findings come from…

item.title: [Critique] World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
item.description:
In an ironic twist, open-source artificial intelligence (AI) platform Hugging Face revealed that it was the victim of a hack perpetrated by an autonomous AI agent system.
The company said it detected and responded to…

item.title: [Faible] SleeperGem Uses Three Malicious RubyGems Packages to Target Developer Machines
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new software supply chain attack codenamed SleeperGem targeting the Ruby ecosystem after three malicious gems were published to RubyGems with the end goal of serving additional…

item.title: [Critique] Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
item.description: F5 has shipped fixes for a critical nginx flaw that lets a remote, unauthenticated attacker trigger a heap buffer overflow in the worker process with crafted HTTP requests. CVE-2026-42533 was patched on July 15 in nginx…

item.title: [Faible] Hackers abuse ViPNet software to target Russian govt agencies
item.description: An advanced threat actor is abusing the update mechanism for the ViPNet private networking product suite to target Russian organizations, including government agencies. [...]

item.title: [Faible] UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
item.description:
Russian state-sponsored threat actors have been observed leveraging the infamous ClickFix strategy to trick Ukrainian targets into infecting their own machines with data-stealing malware.
According to the Computer…

item.title: [Critique] SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
item.description: A previously undocumented threat actor has been attributed to the exploitation of recently disclosed SonicWall Secure Mobile Access (SMA) 1000 series VPN appliances as zero-days prior their public disclosure since June…
